reasoning: The Lean signature matches the informal statement exactly. `[Group G] [Finite G]` captures "finite group G"; `A B : Set G` captures "subsets of G"; `[Nonempty A] [Nonempty B]` on subtypes is equivalent to `A.Nonempty` and `B.Nonempty` ("non-empty subsets"); `A.ncard + B.ncard > (⊤ : Set G).ncard` encodes "|A| + |B| > |G|" because `(⊤ : Set G).ncard = Nat.card G`; and `(⊤ : Set G) = {g | ∃ a ∈ A, ∃ b ∈ B, g = a * b}` is the standard encoding of "G = AB" (saying every g ∈ G can be written a·b). No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the conclusion is logically equivalent to the informal one.
